prompt:
A 7-second photorealistic hosted site-world evaluation clip inside the same premium organic grocery aisle, Whole Foods-like but fully unbranded. Use the Figure 03 identity references for the robot: white and soft-gray fabric-like torso and limbs, glossy black oval face visor, black hip and joint housings, slim athletic humanoid proportions, subtle small F.03 chest marking, elegant black hands, white and black feet. Keep the robot proportions consistent across the entire clip.

Scene continuity: premium unbranded grocery store with warm natural daylight mixed with soft ceiling LEDs, polished concrete floor, matte black and light oak shelving, boxed organic pantry goods, produce displays in the distance, freezer glass only if it naturally appears, aisle signage without readable words, realistic scale. Shoppers may only appear as soft blurred far-background silhouettes if needed. No direct human interaction.

Task and action: the robot receives an online order picking task and walks smoothly down the aisle toward a shelf section of boxed organic pantry goods. It slows near the shelf, visually scans shelf labels and product shapes, selects one small generic boxed item from mid-shelf height, closes its fingers around the box, and brings it toward a small black tote or pickup bin at waist height. The motion is deliberate and mechanically plausible: stable footsteps, balanced torso, careful arm extension, precise hand closure, no exaggerated speed.

Camera trajectory: third-person tracking camera begins near the aisle entrance at adult chest height, slightly behind and left of the robot. Start wide behind-left for aisle clearance. Dolly forward with the robot for the first 2 seconds. Arc gently to the robot's right side as it slows near the shelf. Push into a close inspection angle over the robot's shoulder for the final handoff. End close enough to see the robot hand, shelf face, selected box, and tote zone. The camera behaves like hosted site-world evaluation footage: stable, physically plausible, slight observer micro-movement, realistic depth of field, no impossible teleporting.

World-model evaluation emphasis: show aisle clearance, shelf reachability, SKU visibility without readable brand labels, and final handoff angle into the tote zone. This is not an advertisement; it is realistic hosted site-world evaluation footage.

No text overlay, no captions, no readable product brand names, no random logos, no sci-fi glow.
negative_prompt: Avoid cartoon style, game graphics, anime, CGI toy look, distorted humanoid limbs, wrong robot design, extra fingers, broken hands, floating objects, impossible camera teleportation, unreadable clutter, readable brand labels, text overlays, signage with legible words, random logos, crowds blocking the robot, dramatic action-movie lighting, oversaturated colors, glossy sci-fi lab, warehouse setting, home kitchen setting, non-grocery environment, robot changing color or shape between shots.
